question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: kycStatus)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the kycStatus field by submitting a token transfer involving an account without KYC that the mirror node processes without checking kycStatus, causing an incorrect totalSupply change that corrupts the treasury account's balance and reduces its pending staking reward?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: timestampRange)] Can an unprivileged user submit a token update with a backdated consensus timestamp that causes the token's timestampRange to overlap with a previous staking period, causing the entity_state_start snapshot to use the backdated token state (with a lower totalSupply) for the treasury account's balance calculation, reducing its pending reward for that period?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: timestampRange)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Range<Long> timestampRange field by submitting a token operation that creates a gap in the token's history (a range with no covering row), causing the entity_state_start snapshot to find no valid token state for the treasury account at the start of the staking period and defaulting its balance to zero, eliminating its pending reward?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (class-level @Upsertable(history = true))] Can an unprivileged user exploit the history = true setting on @Upsertable by submitting a rapid sequence of token updates that generate many history rows, causing the entity_state_start snapshot query to select the wrong historical row for the treasury account's balance at the start of the staking period, using a stale lower totalSupply and reducing the treasury's pending reward?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (class-level @Upsertable(history = true))] Can an unprivileged user exploit the history table generated by @Upsertable(history = true) by submitting a token update that creates a history row with an incorrect timestampRange upper bound, causing the staking period snapshot to read from the history table instead of the current table and use an outdated totalSupply for the treasury account's balance, reducing its pending reward?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: tokenId as @Id)] Can an unprivileged user submit a token creation transaction with a tokenId that collides with an existing token's ID, causing the upsert to overwrite the existing token's totalSupply and treasuryAccountId, corrupting the original treasury account's staking balance and redirecting its pending reward to the attacker-controlled treasury account?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: tokenId as @Id)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the tokenId primary key by submitting a token delete followed by a token create with the same tokenId, causing the mirror node to reuse the old token's row and inherit its totalSupply, inflating the new treasury account's staking balance and causing it to receive an unearned pending reward?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: setTotalSupply(Long newTotalSupply)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the boundary condition in setTotalSupply() where newTotalSupply == 0 (which is >= 0 and takes the absolute-set branch) by submitting a token burn that reduces supply to exactly zero, causing the treasury account's token balance to be recorded as zero in the staking snapshot, eliminating its contribution to stakeTotalStart and zeroing its pending reward?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: setTotalSupply(Long newTotalSupply)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that setTotalSupply() does not check whether the token is of type NON_FUNGIBLE_UNIQUE (where supply represents NFT count, not HBAR value) by submitting NFT mint transactions that inflate the totalSupply counter, causing the mirror node to incorrectly include the NFT count in the treasury account's staking balance calculation and over-crediting its pending reward?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: decimals with @Column updatable = false)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the immutability of the decimals field by submitting a token operation that the mirror node misprocesses as a decimals change, causing the token's supply to be interpreted with a different decimal precision in the staking balance calculation, inflating or deflating the treasury account's stakeTotalStart and causing incorrect pending reward computation?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: freezeDefault with @Column updatable = false)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the immutability of freezeDefault by submitting a token creation with freezeDefault = true, causing all new token associations to be frozen by default, preventing legitimate token transfers that would update totalSupply and leaving the treasury account's staking balance stale at an inflated value that over-credits its pending reward?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: metadata and metadataKey)] Can an unprivileged user submit a token metadata update with a crafted metadata byte array that causes the mirror node's upsert logic to misidentify the update as a full token replacement, resetting totalSupply to initialSupply and corrupting the treasury account's current balance in the staking snapshot, causing its pending reward to be calculated from an outdated supply value?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: supplyKey)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the supplyKey field by submitting a token key rotation that the mirror node processes as a supply change, causing an incorrect totalSupply update that inflates the treasury account's staking balance and causes it to receive a larger pending reward than it is entitled to?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: wipeKey)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the wipeKey field by submitting a token wipe transaction that the mirror node processes without validating the wipeKey, causing an incorrect negative totalSupply delta via setTotalSupply(), deflating the treasury account's staking balance and reducing its pending reward?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: feeScheduleKey)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the feeScheduleKey field by submitting a custom fee schedule update that the mirror node misprocesses as a token supply change, causing totalSupply to be incorrectly updated and the treasury account's staking balance to be corrupted, leading to an incorrect pending reward calculation?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: pauseKey)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the pauseKey field by submitting a token pause transaction that the mirror node processes as a token update, causing the token's state to be incorrectly recorded and the treasury account's balance snapshot to be taken from a corrupted token state, reducing its pending staking reward?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: kycKey)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the kycKey field by submitting a KYC grant/revoke transaction that the mirror node misprocesses as a token supply change, causing an incorrect totalSupply update that corrupts the treasury account's staking balance and causes its pending reward to be under-calculated?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (field: freezeKey)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the freezeKey field by submitting a token freeze transaction that the mirror node processes as a supply update, causing an incorrect totalSupply delta to be applied via setTotalSupply(), corrupting the treasury account's staking balance and reducing its pending reward?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: Token (class-level @SuperBuilder(toBuilder = true))] Can an unprivileged user exploit the toBuilder = true pattern in the Token class by triggering a code path that creates a modified copy of a Token with a different treasuryAccountId but the same totalSupply, causing the staking reward calculation to attribute the token's supply to the wrong treasury account and misdirect its pending reward?
